hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with an Operations Management specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Supply Chain Management, Total Quality Management, Project Management, Lean and Six Sigma, and Manufacturing Strategy in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Operations Management: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. Operations depth with Supply Chain Management, TQM, Project Management, Lean and Six Sigma, Manufacturing Strategy, and Service Operations. Strong for manufacturing, logistics, and project management careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Operations Management is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Operations Management specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Supply Chain Management, Total Quality Management, Project Management, Lean and Six Sigma, Manufacturing Strategy, and Service Operations Management. These subjects cover the full operations function from quality systems and lean manufacturing to project execution and supply chain strategy.

LPU's Phagwara, Punjab location gives its alumni access to North India's manufacturing belt spanning Punjab, Haryana, and Himachal Pradesh automotive and pharma clusters. The affordable fee with zero-cost EMI makes LPU Operations MBA the most accessible NIRF-ranked operations management credential for working professionals.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply integrated supply chain frameworks, demand-supply synchronisation, and supply chain risk management for operations roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem3Subjects.description: Implement TQM principles, ISO 9001 quality systems, statistical process control, and quality audit frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Project Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply PMBOK methodology, WBS design, critical path analysis, and project risk management for operations project roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Develop operations-led business strategy, vertical integration analysis, and manufacturing cost leadership frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Operations Management specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present summer training project findings to faculty panel, developing professional communication and analytical skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied operations research project on process improvement, supply chain optimisation, or plant management.

sem4Subjects.name: Lean and Six Sigma
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Green Belt DMAIC tools, value stream mapping, 5S, and lean waste elimination to manufacturing and service operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Manufacturing Strategy
sem4Subjects.description: Design make-vs-buy decisions, capacity planning, plant layout optimisation, and manufacturing network strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Service Operations Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply service design principles, waiting line management, service quality measurement, and capacity management for service operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Operations Management specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Operations Management MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, Pharmaceuticals, Automotive, E-commerce logistics, IT services (delivery operations), Consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Production Supervisor, Quality Analyst, Supply Chain Analyst, Project Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Plant Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Quality Manager, Project Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, COO, Head of Supply Chain, Head of Manufacturing, Operations Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Maruti Suzuki, Hero MotoCorp, Lupin Pharmaceuticals, Sun Pharma, Tata Motors, HUL, Nestle India, Amazon India Operations, Mahindra Logistics, DHL India, Infosys BPO, TCS Operations

skills.body:
The LPU Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing and logistics employers test at operations manager and plant manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Total Quality Management: ISO 9001 implementation, statistical process control, quality audit design, and TQM framework deployment for quality management roles
- Lean and Six Sigma: Green Belt DMAIC tools, value stream mapping, 5S implementation, and lean waste elimination for process improvement roles
- Supply Chain Management: SCOR model application, demand-supply synchronisation, supplier evaluation, and supply chain risk management for operations and procurement roles
- Project Management: PMBOK methodology, WBS design, critical path method, and project risk management for project manager and PMO roles

**Applied skills:**
- Manufacturing Strategy: capacity planning, make-vs-buy analysis, plant layout design, and manufacturing network optimisation for plant and factory management
- Service Operations Management: service capacity management, waiting line optimisation, and service quality measurement for service sector operations

**Soft skills:** Building business cases for process improvement investments, presenting operational KPIs to the COO, leading cross-functional quality improvement teams, and negotiating capacity plans with manufacturing partners.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Operations Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Production and Operations covers manufacturing, quality, and supply chain broadly. LPU Operations Management at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is significantly more affordable. LPU offers zero-cost EMI; Amity does not. For North India manufacturing careers on a budget, LPU Operations is a strong value proposition.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Operations Management:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000. MUJ Operations covers supply chain and quality management with Coursera Plus. LPU Operations Management includes dedicated Lean and Six Sigma, Manufacturing Strategy, and Service Operations Management as distinct Sem 4 subjects. Both are comparable options.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management:** NMIMS Ops and Data Sciences is analytics-heavy with quantitative modelling focus. NMIMS carries stronger Mumbai brand recognition. For data-driven operations and analytics roles, NMIMS is more targeted. For general operations management, manufacturing, and project management roles in North India, LPU Operations Management is a strong and affordable choice.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Operations Management offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Operations Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Operations Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Harjot Singh
items.liked: Lean and Six Sigma and TQM subjects were directly applicable to my manufacturing plant role. Zero-cost EMI made the programme very accessible.
items.disliked: Manufacturing Strategy subject could include more EV and tech manufacturing case studies.
items.body: I had 5 years in pharma manufacturing operations and the LPU Operations MBA helped me move into a plant manager role. The Rs 1.61L fee with zero-cost EMI was the key factor. LPU brand is recognised at North India manufacturing companies.

items.name: Sumit Bansal
items.liked: Project Management and Supply Chain Management were well-structured. Strong practical orientation for manufacturing roles.
items.disliked: Service Operations Management was less relevant for pure manufacturing careers.
items.body: Good programme for operations career advancement in North India. Chandigarh manufacturing companies recognised the LPU credential. For FMCG or automotive operations, this is a strong choice at an affordable price.

items.name: Deepak Malhotra
items.liked: TQM and Lean Six Sigma gave me tools directly applicable to our ISO certification process.
items.disliked: Placement support for senior operations roles was passive.
items.body: Solid credential for North India manufacturing professionals. The zero-cost EMI made it a clear choice. For pan-India operations brand recognition, NMIMS or Amity would carry more weight. For Punjabi manufacturing sector roles, LPU is well-positioned.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Does LPU Operations MBA cover Six Sigma certification?
faqs.answer: LPU's Lean and Six Sigma subject covers Green Belt level DMAIC tools, value stream mapping, and lean waste elimination. This is academic coverage, not a formal Six Sigma certification. For formal Green Belt certification, students should pursue a standalone certification programme alongside the MBA.

faqs.question: Is LPU Operations MBA suitable for pharma manufacturing careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Total Quality Management, Lean and Six Sigma, and Manufacturing Strategy subjects are directly applicable to pharma manufacturing quality and operations roles. LPU's North India alumni network includes placements at Punjab-based pharma companies.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI and are in manufacturing, logistics, pharma, or project management roles in North India.
You want dedicated Lean and Six Sigma, Manufacturing Strategy, and TQM coverage in a single operations MBA.
consider: If analytics-driven operations and quantitative modelling is the focus, NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management offers deeper analytics coverage.
If pan-India manufacturing brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management at Rs 2.25L is well recognised.
If Manipal brand with Coursera Plus is preferred, MUJ Online MBA Operations Management is available at comparable fees.
